description: Declarative hook documentation — catalogs all hooks with event type, purpose, and configuration. The authoritative hook registration remains in hooks.json or settings.json; this file documents and describes the hooks.

hooks.name: prereq-check
hooks.purpose: Validates tool prerequisites (bash, python3, jq, git) and provides rich session context on startup including recovery hints, lifecycle detection, and anchor checks

hooks.name: intent-router
hooks.purpose: Detects project lifecycle state (virgin, discovered, planned, building, checkpoint, complete) and routes natural language user prompts to the appropriate VibeOS skill

hooks.name: secrets-scan
hooks.purpose: Scans file content for secret patterns (API keys, tokens, connection strings) and blocks writes containing secrets

hooks.name: frozen-files
hooks.purpose: Blocks edits to frozen files (CLAUDE.md, .env, and any files listed in frozen-files.json)

hooks.name: test-file-protection
hooks.purpose: Blocks implementation agents (backend, frontend, doc-writer) from modifying test files. Enforces TDD discipline: only tester and test-auditor agents may write tests

hooks.name: test-diff-audit
hooks.purpose: Detects test weakening — removal of assertions, deletion of test functions, addition of skip markers. Warns when test coverage decreases

hooks.name: worktree-scope-guard
hooks.purpose: Enforces exclusive path territories during parallel worktree execution. Reads .vibeos/worktree-scopes.json to prevent cross-branch file modifications

hooks.name: worktree-bash-guard
hooks.purpose: Blocks dangerous git and database operations in parallel worktree branches (no checkout main, no cross-branch merge, no force push, no destructive DB ops)

hooks.name: stub-detection
hooks.purpose: Post-response check for stubs, placeholders, TODOs, incomplete code, and swallowed errors. Flags issues for immediate remediation
